response.justification: The role is fundamentally a senior C++ position requiring 8–12 years of hands-on C++ development, industrial cybersecurity expertise, and IoT/industrial-protocol experience. Bogdan's C++ exposure is limited to a single 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project — effectively negligible for a mid-senior C++ architect role. His career is built primarily on TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web/full-stack engineering, with no evidence of SDL practices, IEC 62443 compliance, or industrial-domain protocols such as MQTT, Modbus, or OPC UA. While his distributed-systems and event-driven background shows genuine engineering depth, it does not bridge the core technical gap this role requires.
strengths: Hands-on high-performance, low-latency distributed-systems experience at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second, async I/O, fibers)
Practical event-driven and message-based architecture through the open-source GISCollective platform
Proven full-stack skills in Node.js, TypeScript, and React — directly matching the JD's listed advantageous technologies
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated — roughly 6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015, with no subsequent C++ work in over a decade
No evidence of industrial cybersecurity knowledge, Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) practices, or IEC 62443 compliance
Zero background in IoT, industrial automation, or domain protocols (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0)
